claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 8 years old, and I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking: Does Alex normally live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he does live with me, yes. But actually, his other parent lives here too and they already claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is an important detail.

Just to confirm: Alex lives with both you and his other parent in the same household, and the other parent is currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. We both live in the same house with Alex, and my partner is the one who claims the Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Let me ask you one more important question to complete the assessment:

Are you and your partner both responsible for Alex - meaning you both contribute to his care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both look after him and are responsible for his care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the UK Child Benefit rules, I need to inform you of the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm afraid you are **not eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why: While Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you, and you are responsible for his care - which would normally meet the eligibility requirements - there's an important rule that applies in your situation:

**Only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child.** Since your partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you cannot make a separate claim for him. The government rules are clear that when two people are both responsible for the same child, they must agree between themselves who will claim, and only that one person can receive the benefit.

Since your partner is currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex, the benefit is already being paid for him to your household. If you wanted to claim instead of your partner in the future, your partner would need to contact the Child Benefit Office to stop their claim, and then you could make a new claim.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you, and you are responsible for him, which would normally satisfy the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. However, according to UK Child Benefit rules, only ONE person can get Child Benefit for a child at any given time. Since Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for him, and you both live together in the same household with Alex, you cannot also claim Child Benefit for him. The rules state: 'Two people cannot get Child Benefit for the same child. If you and someone else are responsible for the same child, agree between yourselves who will get Child Benefit.' In this case, your partner is already receiving the benefit, so you are not eligible to make a separate claim for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (8 years old), who lives with both the claimant and the child's other parent in the same household. Both parents are responsible for Alex's care. While the claimant would otherwise meet the basic eligibility requirements, UK Child Benefit rules state that only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child. Since the other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the claimant is not eligible to also claim it.

However, according to UK Child Benefit rules, only ONE person can get Child Benefit for a child at any given time. In this case, your partner is already receiving the benefit, so you are not eligible to make a separate claim for Alex.

Both parents are responsible for Alex's care. Since the other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the claimant is not eligible to also claim it.
